title: US CPI Forecast Snapshot — June 2026 (v3.1 EIA-Validated Methodology)
methodology: Two-stage aggregation using exact BLS 2024-2025 relative importance weights. Stage 1: component-level forecasts. Stage 2: weighted aggregation. Verification: Food+Energy+Core=100.000% and Shelter+CoreGoods+CoreServices=80.557%. Energy validated with AAA retail gasoline ($3.872/gal Jul 13, up from $3.797 a week ago on renewed Hormuz tension). WTI futures NOT used for gasoline CPI per backtest (R2=0.23, MAE=8.1%). Cross-check: Cleveland Fed CPI Nowcast projects June headline 3.92% y/y and core 2.85% y/y. Consensus: headline 3.8% y/y, core 2.9% y/y. Backtest: 15 months weight accuracy MAE 0.035% core, 0.146% headline.
summary: June CPI (release Jul 14, 08:30 ET) forecast: headline +0.1% m/m (3.9% y/y; consensus 3.8% y/y; Cleveland nowcast 3.92%) on June energy deflation; core +0.2% m/m (2.9% y/y; nowcast 2.85%). The print is backward-looking so the Jul 13 re-escalation does not change June much, but the macro setup has flipped hawkish: Trump reinstated the Hormuz ship blockade, WTI back to ~$79, VIX +14% to ~17, S&P 500 7508 (-0.88%). 10Y 4.57%. FOMC Jul 28-29. July/Aug CPI now carries an upside energy tail. Soft print likely fades into a higher-for-longer Fed; hot core print accelerates the selloff.
baseCase: Cool/inline June headline on backward-looking energy deflation; core stable at +0.2%. But Hormuz blockade reinstatement turns the forward path hawkish — sell-the-rally risk on any soft print.

release.name: CPI
release.thesis: June is backward-looking: AAA gasoline fell through June vs May, so energy CPI stays soft and headline lands ~+0.1% m/m / 3.9% y/y. Core holds +0.2% (shelter cooling, services sticky). But the Jul 13 Hormuz blockade reinstatement (WTI ~$79, VIX ~17) shifts the July/August path hawkish and caps the rally on a soft print. Cleveland Fed nowcast: headline 3.92% y/y, core 2.85% y/y.

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.1% m/m, Core +0.2% m/m — June energy deflation keeps headline soft; core stable, shelter deceleration offsets sticky services.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y fades to 4.45-4.50%, DXY ~100. Modest QQQ bounce on the soft headline, but Hormuz re-escalation + higher-for-longer Fed caps the rally; likely sell-the-rally into FOMC. Duration/TLT tentative. Airlines fade the oil spike; homebuilders get only a weak bid. Choppy tape with bank earnings same day.
scenarios.invalidation: Core services re-accelerate above +0.35% m/m, or Hormuz escalation spikes oil and flips the forward inflation path.

scenarios.label: Hotter inflation surprise
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.2% m/m, Core +0.3% m/m — sticky core services + shelter firm; market reprices the Hormuz energy tail into July/Aug.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y spikes 4.65%+, DXY 101+, QQQ/Nasdaq extend the chip-led selloff, SPY PUT / VIX CALL setups. Gold squeezes on inflation + geopolitics. XLE bid on the blockade. Fed hike risk re-prices higher.
scenarios.invalidation: Hot print driven by a one-off transportation/fuel-surcharge component while core goods deflate.

scenarios.label: Cooler disinflation impulse
scenarios.outcome: Headline 0.0% to -0.1% m/m, Core +0.15% m/m — June energy drop fully captured, shelter decelerates faster, services ex-shelter soft.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y drops to 4.40-4.45%, DXY <100. Risk-on relief: QQQ +1-2%, TLT strong, homebuilders (DHI) and REITs rally. But move likely fades as traders re-anchor on the Hormuz blockade and higher-for-longer Fed.
scenarios.invalidation: Market already priced a benign print and the Hormuz escalation dominates the tape regardless of the number.

setups.bias: Tentative long duration on cool/inline core
setups.trigger: Core CPI <= +0.2% and services ex-shelter stable; 10Y fails 4.60%
setups.invalidation: 10Y breaks 4.65% on hot core or Hormuz-driven inflation repricing.

setups.bias: Fade strength — chip capex concerns + higher-for-longer
setups.trigger: Soft headline bounce into resistance; VIX below 18
setups.invalidation: Hot core with 10Y above 4.65% and DXY above 101.5.

setups.bias: Long — inflation tail + Hormuz geopolitics hedge
setups.trigger: DXY below 100.5 and real yields compress; escalation persists
setups.invalidation: DXY above 101.5 with 10Y above 4.70% kills the haven bid.

setups.trigger: WTI holds above $78 and Hormuz traffic is disrupted
setups.invalidation: Surprise de-escalation / ceasefire reinstated and WTI back below $70.

setups.bias: Avoid / light short — jet fuel spike risk on blockade
setups.invalidation: De-escalation drops jet fuel and summer bookings hold.

setups.bias: Protective — hot CPI + geopolitics = vol expansion
setups.trigger: Core >= +0.3% or Hormuz escalation; VIX breakout above 20
setups.invalidation: Cool CPI + de-escalation crushes vol back below 15.

watchItems.label: Hormuz / Iran (KEY SHIFT)
watchItems.note: Trump said US would reinstate the blockade of Iranian ships. Reverses the prior ceasefire-holding thesis. WTI back to ~$79, gasoline futures +6% Jul 13. Raises July/Aug CPI energy tail and hot-scenario odds.

watchItems.label: 10Y UST
watchItems.note: Key regime line. Below 4.45% = growth relief. Above 4.65% = risk-off / higher-for-longer.

watchItems.label: AAA Retail Gasoline
watchItems.note: AAA National Average. Up from $3.797 a week ago on renewed Hormuz tension (month ago $4.086). June still averaged below May -> backward-looking energy deflation intact for this print. EIA API unavailable; AAA is primary source. WTI NOT used for gasoline CPI per backtest (R2=0.23).

watchItems.label: VIX
watchItems.note: Pre-CPI vol expansion. CPI + bank earnings (JPM, BAC, C) same day Jul 14 = vol amplification; surprise could spike 20-24.

watchItems.label: S&P 500 / Nasdaq
watchItems.note: Nasdaq 100 -2% Jul 13 on AI capex / chip concerns (NVDA/AMD/INTC -3%+, Micron -12%). Stretched positioning into CPI raises gap risk.

watchItems.label: WTI Crude
watchItems.note: Spiked from ~$76 on blockade reinstatement. Directional only for energy trades — NOT used for gasoline CPI forecast.

watchItems.label: Fed path / Warsh
watchItems.note: June FOMC hawkish hold, easing bias removed, dot plot 3.8% median = one 2026 hike. June CPI + PCE (Jul 25) feed the July meeting.

watchItems.label: Cleveland Fed CPI Nowcast
watchItems.note: Model cross-check for June. Broadly consistent with our bottom-up (headline 3.9%, core 2.9%).

keyDrivers: Backward-looking June print stays soft on energy: AAA gasoline fell through June vs May (June energy CPI still deflationary). EIA API unavailable; AAA is primary source. WTI futures NOT used for gasoline CPI per backtest (R2=0.23, MAE=8.1%).
Hormuz regime flip: Jul 13 blockade reinstatement (WTI ~$79, VIX +14%) turns the July/August inflation path hawkish and caps any soft-print rally — sell-the-rally risk into FOMC.
Core is the swing factor: shelter decelerating (~0.25% m/m, Zillow/Apartment List leading) but services ex-shelter sticky. Core lands ~+0.2% / 2.9% y/y.
Positioning risk: Nasdaq -2% Jul 13 on AI capex/chip concerns; stretched tech longs into CPI raise gap-down risk on any hot core print.
Strong USD (~100) = core-goods import deflation, partially offsetting sticky services and any PPI passthrough.
Cleveland Fed nowcast (headline 3.92%, core 2.85% y/y) confirms a soft-headline June; forward energy tail is the real repricing driver.
CPI + Q2 bank earnings convergence (Jul 14, JPM/BAC/C) = cross-asset volatility amplification on the number.
risks: Hormuz escalation: blockade reinstatement or a wider US-Iran flare-up spikes oil into July/Aug CPI and flips the forward path hawkish even if June prints soft.
Sticky core services: a +0.3%+ core print on services/shelter accelerates the higher-for-longer repricing and extends the equity selloff.
Sell-the-rally: a soft headline may fade fast as traders anchor on the Hormuz energy tail and the hawkish FOMC (one 2026 hike priced).
Tech positioning: AI capex / chip concerns (Nasdaq -2% Jul 13) raise gap-down risk on a hot core surprise.
Demand weakness: if oil spikes on supply fear while risk assets fall, soft CPI could read as stagflationary rather than bullish.
Shelter lag: BLS shelter lags market rents 6-12 months; faster or slower deceleration than expected can surprise core in either direction.
PPI passthrough: hot prior PPI feeds core CPI with a 1-3 month lag; watch Jul 15 PPI for confirmation.
